Why Raise Smart School of Technology?

IT Park Ecosystem
Direct connection to real tech companies and startups.
  • Where learning meets industry
  • Raise Smart features an Integrated IT Park that connects students directly with the tech ecosystem.
  • Students work alongside startups, developers, and innovators, gaining practical exposure to real technology development environments.
Build From Day One
Hands-on projects and real-world tech development.
  • Learn tech by creating it
  • At Raise Smart, learning starts with building.
  • From the first semester, students work on real-world projects, applications, and solutions, gaining hands-on experience that prepares them for real careers.
Global & Industry Exposure
Learn from global experts and industry mentors.
  • Learn beyond the classroom
  • Students interact with industry experts, global mentors, and technology leaders through workshops, guest sessions, and collaborative projects.
  • This exposure helps students understand real industry challenges and global technology trends.
Future-Ready AI and Tech Integrated Curriculum
AI-driven learning with modern technologies.
  • Designed for tomorrow’s technologies
  • The curriculum focuses on AI, software engineering, emerging technologies, and innovation, ensuring students graduate with skills relevant to the fast-changing tech world.
Semester Skill Certification Programme
Earn certifications that validate real skills.
  • Build credentials that matter
  • At Raise Smart , every semester is aligned with industry-relevant certification programs.
    Students gain verified skills in areas like programming, AI, cloud, and emerging technologies.
  • These certifications enhance employability and ensure students graduate with a strong professional profile recognized by industry leaders.
Semester Industry Immersion Programme
Experience the industry every semester.
  • Learn by being part of it
  • Students regularly engage with companies through structured industry immersion programs. Each semester includes live projects, company interactions, and practical exposure to real workflows.
  • This continuous immersion bridges the gap between academics and industry expectations.
Field Exposure Programme
Step outside the classroom into the real world.
  • Experience technology in action
  • Students participate in field visits to tech parks, companies, and innovation hubs. They observe real operations, interact with professionals, and understand how technology is implemented in real environments.
  • This exposure builds practical understanding and industry awareness.
Outbound Leadership Training
Build leadership beyond academics.
  • Learn to lead with confidence
  • Through outbound training programs, students develop leadership, teamwork, and decision-making skills. Activities include real-world challenges, group tasks, and experiential learning sessions.
  • These programs help students build confidence, adaptability, and strong interpersonal skills.
MacBook Neo
Empowered with the right technology.
  • Learn with world-class tools
  • Students are equipped with high-performance MacBook devices to support their learning journey. From coding to design and AI development, students work on powerful systems that enhance productivity and creativity.
  • This ensures a seamless, industry-standard tech experience.

At Raise Smart School of Technology, you don't just attend classes you create projects, solve real problems, and become industry-ready.

Raise Smart vs Traditional Tech Education

AITED – AI Tools Exploration & Development

Students explore Artificial Intelligence tools and emerging technologies, experiment with modern platforms, and stay updated with technological trends.

Pro Power Talks – Industry Interaction (Biweekly Sessions)

Industry professionals interact directly with students, sharing real-world insights, career guidance, and exposure to current market demands.

Peer Power Hive – Interest-Based Innovation Teams

Students form teams based on common interests such as Game Design, Game Development, Web Development, and Mobile App Development. They engage in focused evening sessions, exploring the latest tools under faculty mentorship.

Peer Power Talks – Students Empowering Students

Students share their knowledge, competition experiences, and new learnings with peers, strengthening communication and collaborative learning.

FREAQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

General Questions — Undergraduate (UG)

Why should I choose engineering as a career path?

Engineering is the #1 career choice for job security, salary, and global impact in the 21st century. Engineering roles are projected to grow 14% globally by 2030, faster than most other professions (U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics).

The average starting salary for engineering graduates in India ranges from ₹4–8 LPA, with top performers in AI and cybersecurity earning ₹15–25 LPA within 3 years. Globally, engineering consistently ranks in the top 5 highest-paid degree categories.

Every device, app, bridge, and system you interact with daily was designed by an engineer. As AI, automation, and digital transformation accelerate, engineers who build these systems will be the most in-demand professionals on the planet.

At RAISE SMART, students build real technology from semester one — graduating with a portfolio, not just a degree.

Which engineering branch is best for the future?

The top 3 future-proof engineering branches are Computer Science & AI/ML, Cybersecurity, and AI & Data Science — all driven by multi-trillion-dollar global demand. The global AI market is forecast to reach $1.8 trillion by 2030 (Grand View Research). Cybersecurity job openings globally exceed 3.5 million unfilled roles annually (Cybersecurity Ventures).

Other high-demand branches include Cloud & DevOps, Robotics & Automation, Biomedical Engineering, and Electrical & Electronics Engineering (especially for EV and renewable energy). Even traditional fields like Civil and Mechanical Engineering are surging — smart cities and AI-assisted design are creating new demand.

  • CSE / AI / ML — Highest-paying globally; powers every major tech product
  • Cybersecurity — 3.5M+ unfilled roles globally; critical shortage = top salaries
  • Data Science — Every Fortune 500 company now hires data professionals
  • Cloud & DevOps — 100% of digital businesses run on cloud infrastructure
  • Robotics — Manufacturing and healthcare automation expanding at 15%+ CAGR

All high-demand branches are offered at RAISE SMART, aligned to live industry hiring trends.

What are the top engineering courses for the future?

The highest-value engineering programmes in 2025–2030 are B.E./B.Tech CSE, CSE with AI/ML, CSE with Cybersecurity, and B.Tech AI & Data Science. India alone needs 1 million+ AI and data science professionals by 2026 (NASSCOM). Global cloud computing jobs are growing at 22% per year (LinkedIn Emerging Jobs Report).

Top programmes by career outcome and global demand:

  • B.E. CSE / B.Tech CSE — Foundation of all tech roles; opens every door in software, AI, and product engineering
  • CSE with AI & ML — Fastest-growing specialisation globally; average salary 40–60% higher than general CSE
  • CSE with Cybersecurity — Critical global shortage; ethical hackers and security analysts among top-paid tech roles
  • B.Tech AI & Data Science — Every industry now requires data professionals; median salary ₹8–18 LPA within 3 years
  • ECE / EEE — Essential for IoT, 5G, EV, and renewable energy — all growing sectors
  • Robotics & Automation — Industrial automation market worth $400B+ globally by 2030
  • Biomedical Engineering — Healthcare AI and medtech sector growing at 25%+ annually

All these programmes are available at RAISE SMART with hands-on, project-based delivery.

Is engineering hard to study?

Engineering is challenging — but the difficulty is manageable with the right teaching method. Most students struggle because of how it's taught, not because of the subject itself. Studies show that students in project-based learning environments score 20–30% higher on application-based assessments and report significantly higher engagement (Stanford d.school Research, 2022).

Traditional lecture-based engineering education disconnects theory from application. When you learn why a concept matters and apply it immediately through real projects, difficulty drops and confidence grows fast.

At RAISE SMART, every concept is applied the same week it is taught — through hands-on tasks, real projects, and industry mentor feedback. You are never stuck with theory you don’t understand how to use.

Why should I choose an MBA?

An MBA accelerates your path to leadership, increases your earning potential, and gives you the business and entrepreneurial framework to compete at the highest level. MBA graduates earn 50–80% more than their non-MBA peers within 10 years of graduation (Graduate Management Admission Council, GMAC 2023 Report). Top MBA holders in tech-management roles in India command ₹25–60 LPA packages.

The MBA remains one of the most ROI-positive qualifications in the world — especially when combined with technology management. As AI and digital transformation reshape every industry, tech-fluent MBA holders are among the most sought-after professionals globally.

  • Career acceleration — MBA graduates reach senior roles 3–5 years faster on average
  • Salary premium — 50–80% higher lifetime earnings vs non-MBA peers (GMAC)
  • Entrepreneurial edge — Business framework to launch, fund, and scale ventures
  • Industry versatility — MBA skills apply across tech, finance, healthcare, and beyond

RAISE SMART’s MBA integrates AI tools and technology management into every business discipline.

What is the future of management as a career?

The future of management is tech-fluent, data-driven, and human-centred. Managers who understand AI, analytics, and digital strategy will lead — those who don't will be managed by those who do. The World Economic Forum projects that 85 million jobs will be displaced by automation by 2025 — but 97 million new roles will be created, most requiring management and digital skills combined (WEF Future of Jobs Report 2023).

AI is not replacing managers — it is raising the bar for what great management looks like. Managers who can lead remote teams, interpret data dashboards, build ESG strategies, and drive digital transformation will be the most valuable professionals of the next two decades.

  • Tech-fluent leadership — AI literacy is now a core management competency
  • Data-driven decisions — Managers who use analytics make decisions 5x faster with 80% higher accuracy
  • ESG & sustainability — Over 90% of S&P 500 companies now publish ESG reports; sustainability managers in critical demand
  • Remote team leadership — Over 60% of knowledge work is now hybrid or remote (McKinsey, 2023)
What are the top MBA specialisations for the future?

The top future-relevant MBA specialisations are Business Analytics & AI, Digital Marketing, FinTech, Sustainability Management, and AI Product Management. The global business analytics market is projected to reach $650 billion by 2029 (Fortune Business Insights). Demand for AI product managers grew 35% year-on-year in 2023 (LinkedIn Emerging Jobs Report).

Top MBA specialisations by career demand and salary potential:

  • MBA Business Analytics & AI — Most in-demand specialisation; data leaders earn ₹20–50 LPA in top firms
  • MBA Digital Marketing & E-Commerce — Global digital ad spend exceeds $700B annually; every brand needs digital marketing leaders
  • MBA FinTech & Financial Management — FinTech sector growing at 20% CAGR; digital banking and blockchain expertise in high demand
  • MBA Sustainability Management — Net-zero commitments by 2030 creating thousands of senior ESG roles globally
  • MBA AI Product Management — Among the highest-paid MBA roles in tech; combines strategy with AI product delivery
  • MBA Entrepreneurship 5.0 — AI-first ventures and platform businesses — the fastest-growing startup model globally
  • MBA Sports Management — Global sports industry worth $500B+; broadcasting, tech, and fan engagement driving new roles

All specialisations are offered at RAISE SMART with AI-tool integration across every module.

What is the future of Artificial Intelligence?

AI is the most transformative technology in human history. By 2030, it is projected to add $15.7 trillion to the global economy — more than the current output of China and India combined. PwC projects AI will contribute $15.7 trillion to the global economy by 2030. The global AI market is growing at a CAGR of 37% (Grand View Research, 2024).

AI is actively reshaping every industry right now:

  • Engineering — AI tools like GitHub Copilot are now used by 1.3 million+ developers; AI-powered simulation reduces design cycles by up to 70%
  • Healthcare — AI diagnostic systems detect certain cancers with 94%+ accuracy vs 88% for human radiologists (Nature Medicine, 2023)
  • Finance — AI fraud detection systems analyse 1 million+ transactions per second; banks save billions annually
  • Manufacturing — Smart factories using AI predictive maintenance reduce downtime by 30–50% (McKinsey)
  • Agriculture — AI-powered precision farming increases crop yields by 15–20% while reducing water use
  • Education — AI tutoring systems improve learning outcomes by up to 2x compared to traditional classroom instruction

At RAISE SMART, AI is not a subject — it is woven into every programme from day one.

What AI skills should engineering students learn?

The 3 most valuable AI skill areas for engineering students in 2025 are Python + ML Frameworks, Cloud AI Deployment, and LLM/Generative AI development. Python is used by 70%+ of all AI/ML professionals globally (Stack Overflow Developer Survey 2023). Engineers with cloud AI deployment skills earn 35–50% more than those without (LinkedIn Salary Insights).

Priority AI skills by category:

  • Foundation — Python, NumPy, Pandas — 100% of AI roles require these
  • Core ML — TensorFlow / PyTorch — used in 90%+ of production AI systems
  • Generative AI — Hugging Face, LangChain, OpenAI API — fastest-growing skill set in 2024–25
  • Computer Vision — OpenCV, YOLO — used in healthcare, security, and autonomous vehicles
  • Deployment — Docker, AWS SageMaker, Google Vertex AI — production-grade AI skills
  • Coding Productivity — GitHub Copilot — already standard in 60%+ of tech companies

Start with Python + Jupyter + Scikit-learn. Then add deep learning, deployment tools, and specialised libraries as you build real projects.

At RAISE SMART’s AI Skills Lab (inaugurated by Dell & Intel), all these tools are available from semester one.

What AI skills should MBA and management students learn?

Management students don't need to code — but mastering AI-powered business tools will make you 40–60% more productive and significantly more hireable. Executives who use AI tools regularly make decisions 5x faster and with higher accuracy (Harvard Business Review, 2023). Microsoft Copilot users report 30% productivity gains on average (Microsoft 2024 Work Trend Index).

Top AI tools every management student must know:

  • Strategy & Research — Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity AI, Consensus AI — for analysis, summarisation, and decision support
  • Data & Analytics — Power BI, Tableau, Google Looker Studio — turn raw data into actionable business insights
  • Marketing & Sales — HubSpot AI, Salesforce Einstein, Jasper AI — automate campaigns, scoring, and content
  • Finance & Operations — Zoho Analytics, SAP AI, Oracle AI — financial planning, risk management, and supply chain
  • HR & People — Workday AI, Darwinbox, HireVue — workforce planning, screening, and performance management
  • Productivity — Microsoft Copilot, Notion AI, Otter.ai, Gamma AI — daily work automation

RAISE SMART’s MBA curriculum integrates all of these tools into live business simulations and real projects.

What AI tools should engineering students learn?

Engineering students who master Python, TensorFlow/PyTorch, cloud AI platforms, and GitHub Copilot will have a measurable edge in placements and career growth. Engineers with AI/ML skills earn 25–45% higher starting salaries. Demand for ML engineers grew 74% year-on-year (LinkedIn, 2023). 98% of Fortune 500 companies now use cloud AI platforms.

Essential AI tools by category:

  • ML Frameworks — TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, Keras — core of every AI/ML role
  • Data Tools — Pandas, NumPy, Jupyter, Google Colab — standard in all data science work
  • NLP & LLMs — Hugging Face, LangChain, OpenAI API — fastest-growing engineering skill set
  • Computer Vision — OpenCV, YOLO, Roboflow — used in autonomous systems, healthcare, and security
  • Dev Tools — GitHub Copilot, VS Code AI, FastAPI — standard in modern engineering teams
  • Cloud AI — AWS SageMaker, Google Vertex AI, Azure AI — enterprise-scale deployment skills

Don’t try to learn everything at once. Start with Python and Scikit-learn, build projects, then go deeper into your chosen domain.

At RAISE SMART’s AI Skills Lab, students access all these tools through structured real-world project work.

What AI tools should MBA and management students specifically use for daily work?

The 5 most impactful AI tools for MBA students right now are Claude, Power BI, Salesforce Einstein, Microsoft Copilot, and HubSpot AI. Professionals who regularly use AI productivity tools complete tasks 37% faster on average (McKinsey, 2024). CRM platforms with AI (like Salesforce Einstein) improve sales forecast accuracy by up to 40%.

  • Claude / ChatGPT — Research, strategy documents, and business writing — already used in boardrooms globally
  • Power BI / Tableau — Turn raw data into visual insights; used by 80%+ of Fortune 500 companies
  • Microsoft Copilot — AI embedded in Word, Excel, Teams — 30% productivity gain for regular users
  • Salesforce Einstein — AI-powered CRM; reduces sales cycle time by up to 30%
  • Canva AI / Adobe Firefly — Create professional marketing assets in minutes, no design background needed
What is RAISE SMART School of Technology?

RAISE SMART School of Technology is India's leading tech-first upskilling and knowledge partner for universities — where students build real technology from day one, not just study it. Industry data shows that graduates from project-based, industry-integrated programmes are 2–3x more likely to receive placement offers within 6 months vs traditional college graduates (NASSCOM Talent Report 2023).

Unlike traditional colleges, RAISE SMART students are physically located inside Rathinam IT Park, surrounded by 50+ active tech companies. Every semester combines accredited academic learning with real-world projects, industry mentorship, and hands-on technology building.

Is RAISE SMART School of Technology a college or university?

RAISE SMART is not a standalone college — it is a premier upskilling and knowledge partner that works with accredited universities to deliver industry-first education alongside your degree.

Students earn a fully accredited academic degree from the partner university AND receive the advanced, industry-aligned training that RAISE SMART delivers. You get the best of both worlds: academic credentials plus real-world experience.

What are the key benefits of joining RAISE SMART School of Technology?

RAISE SMART offers 12+ proven advantages that traditional engineering colleges don't — from AI Skills Labs and industry mentors to MacBooks for every student and live company projects from semester one. Students who do internships or live projects during their degree are 70% more likely to receive a job offer before graduation (NASSCOM Hiring Trends Report 2023).

Key differentiators:

  • Competency-Based Learning — Skills tested by doing, not memorising — every concept applied immediately
  • Real-World Projects — Live company projects from semester one; build a portfolio employers can verify
  • Industry Mentors — Professionals with 10+ years experience mentor you every semester inside the IT Park
  • AI Skills Lab — State-of-the-art lab inaugurated by Dell & Intel for real AI project work
  • MacBook for Every Student — Industry-standard hardware with all tools pre-configured from day one
  • IT Park Campus — Located inside Rathinam IT Park alongside 50+ active tech companies
  • Career Readiness from Semester 1 — Resume, LinkedIn, mock interviews — not added on at the end
  • International Exposure — Global immersion programmes and internationally recognised certifications
  • Startup Incubation — Full support to launch your startup during your degree
What job opportunities and salary packages can I expect after graduating from RAISE SMART?

RAISE SMART graduates have been placed in roles paying up to ₹3 Crore per annum, with multiple students securing packages between ₹45 LPA and ₹58 LPA. Industry data shows AI, cybersecurity, and data science professionals are among the top 5 highest-paid roles in India's tech sector, with median salaries growing 18–22% year-on-year (NASSCOM, 2024).

The placement advantage comes from RAISE SMART’s unique position inside a live IT Park — students are building relationships with 50+ tech companies throughout their degree, not just in their final year.

  • Highest CTC recorded — ₹3 Crore per annum
  • Multiple students placed — ₹45 LPA to ₹58 LPA
  • Strong pipeline — AI, Data Science, and Cybersecurity placement focus

Career readiness begins in semester one — resume, LinkedIn, portfolio, and mock interviews are built throughout the programme.

What facilities does RAISE SMART School of Technology offer?

RAISE SMART provides professional-grade facilities that mirror a real tech company environment — including an AI Skills Lab (Dell & Intel), MacBook for every student, IT Park campus, and structured internships every semester. Students who have access to industry-grade tools during their education are 3x more likely to be job-ready on day one of employment (World Economic Forum Skills Report, 2023).

  • IT Park Campus — 50+ active tech companies on campus — real corporate exposure every day
  • MacBook for Every Student — Pre-configured with all industry tools from day one
  • AI Skills Lab (Dell & Intel) — High-performance computing for real AI project development
  • Digital Library — Curated AI, cybersecurity, and cloud resources tailored to your programme
  • Live Project Workspace — Peer Power Hive — collaborative, corporate-style environment
  • Internships Every Semester — Not just final year — structured internships built into the entire programme
  • International Trips — Global immersion and internationally recognised certifications
What is the culture like at RAISE SMART School of Technology?

RAISE SMART has a professional-first, AI-powered, innovation-driven culture — more like a tech company than a traditional college campus. Research shows that students in industry-integrated learning environments develop professional confidence 2x faster than those in traditional academic settings (Education Research International, 2022).

Culture pillars at RAISE SMART:

  • AI-First Learning — AI tools are used every day across every subject — not just in dedicated AI classes
  • Professional Work Culture — Corporate norms, team collaboration, and delivery standards from day one
  • International Community — Diverse peer community and global exposure programmes
  • Innovation & Entrepreneurship — Active startup incubation culture with real funding pathways
  • Mentor-Driven Growth — Personal mentorship from industry professionals, not just academic lecturers
  • Hackathon & Corporate Challenges — Regular competitions that mirror real industry problem-solving
What is the fee structure at RAISE SMART School of Technology?

RAISE SMART's fee is an investment with proven returns — graduates have secured packages up to ₹3 Crore per annum, and RSTNAT scholarships offer up to 100% fee waiver based on merit. On average, technology graduates from India's top upskilling institutions recover their full education investment within 18–24 months of employment (NASSCOM Skills Outlook, 2023).

Contact the RAISE SMART admissions team for a personalised fee breakdown, scholarship options, and flexible payment plans tailored to your situation.

Are scholarships available at RAISE SMART School of Technology?

Yes — RAISE SMART offers scholarships up to 100% fee waiver through the RSTNAT national entrance exam. Your All India Rank directly determines your scholarship slab. Industry data shows that merit-based scholarship recipients outperform their peers by 25–30% in placement outcomes — a strong correlation between early achievement and career success.

Simply register for RSTNAT, earn your All India Rank, and your scholarship is automatically assigned. The higher your rank, the bigger your scholarship — your merit pays for your future.

What is RSTNAT?

RSTNAT (RAISE SMART National Aptitude Test) is India's Career-First Technology Talent Test — the official gateway to RAISE SMART School of Technology, where your All India Rank unlocks life-changing scholarships. Students who take structured national entrance exams are 40% more likely to have a clear career pathway within their first year of college (Education Research India, 2023).

RSTNAT does more than test academic knowledge — it identifies future tech leaders and rewards them with scholarships that can cover up to 100% of fees. It gives you a competitive edge before traditional college admissions even begin.

Is RSTNAT tougher than JEE or CAT?

No — RSTNAT is significantly more accessible than JEE or CAT. It tests aptitude, foundational science or business knowledge, and tech awareness — not years of intensive preparation. Unlike JEE (top 0.5% qualify) or CAT (scores needed above 99 percentile for top IIMs), RSTNAT is designed to identify motivated, curious learners — not just exam-preparation specialists.

If you are passionate about technology and have a growth mindset, RSTNAT is absolutely crackable with focused preparation. The FREE MOCK TEST is the best way to benchmark yourself before exam day.

What do I need to prepare for RSTNAT?

RSTNAT requires preparation in three focused areas: Aptitude, core subject knowledge (Science or Business), and Tech Awareness — all at a foundational, manageable level. Students who attempt at least 3 practice tests before a national entrance exam improve their scores by an average of 15–25% (Educational Testing Service Research, 2023).

For MBA Programme preparation:

  • Aptitude — Logical reasoning, data interpretation, verbal ability, and critical reasoning — business decision-making focus
  • Business — Marketing fundamentals, microeconomics basics, financial literacy, entrepreneurship, and current business affairs
  • Tech Awareness — AI in business, FinTech, digital marketing, e-commerce, cloud basics, cybersecurity awareness

The smartest preparation strategy: Take the FREE MOCK TEST first, identify your weak areas, then focus your revision there. Students who attempt the mock test walk in with zero surprises.

How long is the RSTNAT exam?

The RSTNAT exam duration is 90 minutes — a focused, structured test designed to assess aptitude and knowledge efficiently without unnecessary length. Research shows that 90-minute exam formats are optimal for sustained cognitive performance — long enough to assess depth, short enough to minimise fatigue effects (Journal of Educational Psychology, 2021).

The 90-minute window rewards preparation and time management. Practice with the FREE MOCK TEST in timed conditions to build the pacing strategy that protects your score.

How many questions are in RSTNAT?

The RSTNAT MBA Programme has 100 questions across three sections — Aptitude, Business, and Tech Awareness — all scenario-based and analytical in format. The 100-question, 90-minute format means roughly 54 seconds per question — placing a premium on preparation, quick thinking, and strategic time management.

Take the FREE MOCK TEST to experience the exact question format, section structure, and timing before exam day. One registration gives unlimited practice attempts.

Are mock tests available for RSTNAT?

Yes — a FREE MOCK TEST is available that exactly mirrors the real RSTNAT exam in format, section structure, timing, and difficulty level. Students who practise with full-length mock tests under timed conditions improve their final exam performance by 15–25% on average (ETS Research, 2023).

  • Real exam pattern & question style — No surprises on exam day
  • Section-wise practice — Aptitude, Business, and Tech Awareness sections
  • Timed experience — Identical to actual exam conditions
  • Instant results — Section-wise performance analysis after every attempt
  • Unlimited attempts — Register once, practise as many times as you want

Register FREE → practice with negative marking enabled → identify weak areas → improve with every attempt.

What is the pass mark and scoring system for RSTNAT?

RSTNAT uses a Performance Band System for the MBA programme — 4 bands from Foundation to Distinguished, with Band 4 (71–100) unlocking the highest scholarship slab. Performance band systems are proven to reduce exam anxiety while improving motivation — students focus on improvement rather than a single pass/fail threshold (Journal of Applied Testing Technology, 2022).

  • Band 1 — Foundation — Score 1–15 · Entry level · Your RAISE SMART journey begins here
  • Band 2 — Proficient — Score 16–30 · Strong foundational thinking and business awareness
  • Band 3 — Advanced — Score 31–70 · Sharp analytical thinking and solid business decision-making
  • Band 4 — Distinguished — Score 71–100 · Exceptional aptitude — highest scholarship slab eligible

Every student who takes RSTNAT takes the first step toward a career-first future at RAISE SMART, regardless of their band.

Is there negative marking in RSTNAT?

Yes — RSTNAT has negative marking. Correct answers earn marks; wrong answers carry a deduction. This rewards accuracy and careful thinking over random guessing. In exams with negative marking, students who develop a deliberate 'attempt vs skip' strategy score 10–20% higher than those who guess randomly (GATE/CAT Strategy Research, 2022).

The golden rule: attempt what you know, skip what you don’t.

The FREE MOCK TEST includes negative marking enabled — so you can practice building the smart exam strategy that protects your score before it counts on actual exam day.

What are the scholarship details for RSTNAT?

RSTNAT offers 4 scholarship slabs based on All India Rank — ranging from 25% fee waiver (Rank 501–1000) up to a full 100% fee waiver (Rank 1–50). On average, merit scholarship recipients in India's top tech institutions report 30% higher career satisfaction and 20% faster promotion rates — high achievers earn and grow faster (NASSCOM, 2023).

  • Gold Scholarship (Rank 1–50) — 100% full fee waiver
  • Silver Scholarship (Rank 51–200) — 75% major fee waiver
  • Bronze Scholarship (Rank 201–500) — 50% half fee waiver
  • Merit Scholarship (Rank 501–1000) — 25% fee waiver

Every student receives a detailed scorecard with subject-wise performance and All India Rank after the exam. Scholarship is automatically assigned — no separate application needed.

The higher your rank, the bigger your scholarship. Your merit pays for your future.

AEO/GEO/AIO/SXO - General Questions — Undergraduate (UG)

What do I need to prepare for RSTNAT?
Subject Important Topics Covered
Physics Simple Harmonic Motion, Waves, Interference, EM Waves, Modern Physics
Chemistry Atomic Structure, Thermodynamics, Electrochemistry, Organic Chemistry, Solid State
Mathematics Limits, Matrices, Vectors, Probability, Trigonometry, Calculus
Tech Awareness Computer Basics, Networking, Binary System, Internet & Security
Aptitude Percentages, Ratio, Time & Work, Speed & Distance, Profit & Loss, Averages
Communication Reading Comprehension, Grammar, Vocabulary, Sentence Correction
Programming Basics Loops, Conditions, Logic Building, Output Prediction
How many questions are in RSTNAT?
Section Subject Marks No. of Questions
Core Science Physics 10 10
Chemistry 10 10
Mathematics 15 15
Tech Awareness 5 5
Subtotal 40 40
Skills & Readiness Aptitude 25 25
Communication 15 15
Programming Basics 20 20
Subtotal 60 60
Total — 100 100 Questions
What is the pass mark and scoring system for RSTNAT?

Option A : RST All India Ranking based

Rank Range Scholarship Category Name Department Allocation
1 – 50 100% RST Elite / Diamond AI & DS, AI & ML
51 – 200 75% RST Prime / Platinum AI & ML, Cyber Security
201 – 500 50% RST Achiever / Gold Cyber Security, CSE
501 – 1000 25% RST Performer / Silver CSE / Related Branches

Option B : Marks and Band Based

Marks Range (Out of 100) Scholarship Category Name Department Allocation
90 – 100 100% RST Elite / Diamond AI & DS, AI & ML
75 – 89 75% RST Prime / Platinum AI & ML, Cyber Security
60 – 74 50% RST Achiever / Gold Cyber Security, CSE
40 – 59 25% RST Performer / Silver CSE / Related Branches
Is there negative marking in RSTNAT?
  • RSTNAT follows a no negative marking system.
  • Each correct answer = +1 mark
  • Each incorrect answer = 0 marks
  • Unattempted questions also give 0 marks
